report.summary: Atlas plugin routing spine audit: 9 core skills all exist and span the intended architecture, but the first 2 entry points (atlas-architect, atlas-engine) plus a third skill (atlas-stacks) are absent from the capability-routing.md Step 2 routing table. 13 agents are completely unrouted from commands (though 12 are wired to skills internally). One agent (api-usage-map) is completely orphaned with no references anywhere. All 18 commands map successfully to at least one skill, with /atlas command acting as the main hub.

findings.claim: Spine entry point atlas-architect missing from routing table (Step 2, rows 16-38)
findings.evidence: atlas-architect skill exists at /Users/jerry/.claude/plugins/cache/tech-tools/atlas/2.6.0/skills/atlas-architect/SKILL.md; plugin.json line 4 names it 'atlas-architect (boot/discovery)'. Zero mentions in capability-routing.md Step 2 routing table. Only defined in plugin.json metadata, not in routing decisions.

findings.claim: Spine orchestrator atlas-engine missing from routing table task signals
findings.evidence: atlas-engine skill exists at /Users/jerry/.claude/plugins/cache/tech-tools/atlas/2.6.0/skills/atlas-engine/SKILL.md (core orchestration layer per plugin.json line 4). Not found in Step 2 routing table rows 16-38. Referenced in 17 commands but no routing entry for 'orchestration' or similar task signal.

findings.claim: atlas-stacks skill missing from routing table
findings.evidence: atlas-stacks skill exists at /Users/jerry/.claude/plugins/cache/tech-tools/atlas/2.6.0/skills/atlas-stacks/SKILL.md. Plugin.json describes it as 'AskUserQuestion-driven skill stacking'. Not in Step 2 routing rows 16-38. Referenced in atlas-stacks.md command but no routing entry for stacking tasks.

findings.claim: Agent api-usage-map is completely orphaned—no command references, no skill references, not in routing
findings.evidence: File exists: /Users/jerry/.claude/plugins/cache/tech-tools/atlas/2.6.0/agents/api-usage-map.md. grep -r 'api-usage-map' /commands/ = 0 results. grep -r 'api-usage-map' /skills/ = 0 results. Not mentioned in capability-routing.md. Candidate for removal.

findings.claim: 12 agents unrouted from commands (not referenced in any /commands/*.md file)
findings.evidence: Agents without command dispatch: completeness-critic, docs-auditor, docs-curator, naming-glossary-audit, planner, rls-privilege-audit, schema-inventory, ux-accuracy-oracle, ux-cartographer, ux-fuzzer, ux-persona, ux-reporter. Each verified with grep -r 'agentname' /commands/ = 0. Only 5 agents have command references: atlas:explorer, atlas:implementer, atlas:db-prober, atlas:ui-runtime-tester, atlas:verifier. However, 12 of 13 unrouted agents ARE referenced in skills (only api-usage-map is completely orphaned).

findings.claim: Skill atlas-architect not dispatched from any command
findings.evidence: grep -r 'atlas-architect' /Users/jerry/.claude/plugins/cache/tech-tools/atlas/2.6.0/commands/ = 0 matches. All other 8 atlas skills routed via at least one command. atlas-architect only discoverable via plugin entry point and capability-routing (which omits it from Step 2 table).

findings.claim: Routing table references external/global agent types not in atlas /agents/ directory
findings.evidence: Line 18 of capability-routing.md mentions: 'codebase-explorer', 'Explore', 'Plan'. None of these exist in /agents/ (18 agents listed; no match). These appear to be global agent types (from external plugins or ~/.claude/agents/). Routing should clarify which are atlas-specific vs external.

unverified: Whether atlas-architect and atlas-engine are intentionally omitted from Step 2 routing because they are bootstrap-only / not user-callable directly. To verify: read atlas-architect/SKILL.md and atlas-engine/SKILL.md entry conditions.

Whether the 12 unrouted agents are invoked dynamically or via internal skill dispatch. To verify: trace each skill's SKILL.md to see how these agents are actually called (may be event-driven or internal).
Whether api-usage-map is a removal candidate or invoked via dynamic agent loading. To verify: read api-usage-map.md file content and search for programmatic references.

Whether external agent types (Explore, Plan, codebase-explorer) reference real agents in ~/.claude/agents/ or other installed plugins. To verify: check ~/.claude/agents/ and run 'ls' to see installed external agents.
report.next_step: 1. Add rows to capability-routing.md Step 2 for atlas-architect (e.g., 'Boot a new project') and atlas-engine (e.g., 'Internal orchestration—do not route directly'). 2. Verify and document the purpose of api-usage-map; delete if unused. 3. Add reference catalog in atlas-engine/references/ that lists unrouted agents and their internal consumers. 4. Reconcile external agent type names with actual available agents and clarify which are atlas-specific vs global.

report.summary: Two UX-testing substrates exist in the atlas plugin with duplicated phases, gates, and logic. Substrate A (atlas-expedition skill) is the canonical, actively-routed entry point. Substrate B (atlas-engine with ux-test-swarm.md reference) defines five named agents that are declared but unrouted.

findings.claim: Substrate A (atlas-expedition) is the actively-routed canonical entry point for UX testing
findings.evidence: capability-routing.md:23 routes 'Full UI/UX test pass / persona testing / pre-release UX sweep' to 'atlas-expedition (canonical home; auto-discovers routes and fields)'; commands/atlas.md:115 lists 'atlas-expedition app-discovering UX runtime test swarm' as the UX test tool

findings.claim: Substrate B agents (ux-cartographer, ux-persona, ux-fuzzer, ux-accuracy-oracle, ux-reporter) are unrouted and explicitly superseded
findings.evidence: ux-test-swarm.md:3-7 states 'CANONICAL HOME: The current, maintained runbook for the UX test swarm is the atlas-expedition skill. Use /atlas-expedition (or dispatch atlas-expedition) for all new UX swarm runs. This file is retained as an engine-orchestration reference'. Agents listed in atlas-engine/SKILL.md:150 but no capability-routing entry; no commands dispatch them

findings.claim: Both substrates implement identical 6-phase lifecycle and 3-gate validation model
findings.evidence: Substrate A (atlas-expedition/SKILL.md:60-65) phases: 0-Discover, 1-Generate, 2-Enter data, 3-Walk UI, 4-Fuzz, 5-Verify accuracy, 6-Synthesize. Substrate B (ux-test-swarm.md:31-39) phases identical. Gates: G1 client-surface success (SKILL.md:159, ux-test-swarm.md:85), G2 evidence-complete (SKILL.md:161-164, ux-test-swarm.md:86), G3 accuracy (SKILL.md:166-169, ux-test-swarm.md:87-97)

findings.claim: Substrate A uses 6 role files as general-purpose subagent prompts; Substrate B references 5 named agents for same phases
findings.evidence: Substrate A roles (atlas-expedition/references/roles/): cartographer.md, scripted-persona.md, browser-persona.md, fuzz-boundary.md, calc-verifier.md, synthesis-reporter.md (6 roles). Substrate B agents (atlas-engine/SKILL.md:150 naming, agents/* files): ux-cartographer, ux-persona, ux-fuzzer, ux-accuracy-oracle, ux-reporter (5 agents). Persona phase covered by 2 roles in A (scripted-persona.md + browser-persona.md) vs 1 agent in B (ux-persona)

findings.claim: Substrate A references missing harness infrastructure (docs/claude_testers/harness/, run_all.sh, datagen.py)
findings.evidence: atlas-expedition/SKILL.md:23 'writes are under docs/claude_testers/harness/'; line 35 maps env knobs to 'run_all.sh'; line 185 runs 'COUNT=12 COVERAGE=standard ... bash run_all.sh'. personas.md:73-79 expects 'datagen.py' at 'RUN_DIR'. None of these files exist in repo (Jun 25 observation confirms 'datagen.py Generator Missing from atlas-expedition')

findings.claim: Capability-routing.md sends all UX work to atlas-expedition, not atlas-engine
findings.evidence: capability-routing.md:23 single entry for UI/UX testing: 'orchestrator dispatches atlas-expedition' with model sonnet. No atlas-engine entry; no agent-dispatch routing for ux-* agents anywhere in routing table

unverified: Whether atlas:ux-* agents were ever used in prior runs or if they are purely aspirational/legacy (agents exist but were never dispatched)
Status of the missing harness infrastructure (run_all.sh, datagen.py) - whether it is being developed separately, abandoned, or moved to another location
Whether atlas-engine skill ever actually dispatches the ux-* agents in practice (no dispatch code found in SKILL.md beyond listing them in line 150)
Installation-time difference: whether installed atlas plugin (~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json) includes or resolves these agents differently than the repo copy
report.next_step: Substrate A (atlas-expedition) is the only actively-routed UX testing entry point. Substrate B agents remain undispelled documentation references. No deletion recommended per task rules, but atlas-engine SKILL.md line 150 should clarify that ux-test-swarm.md is a reference-only document and atlas-expedition is the sole dispatcher. Missing harness infrastructure blocks Substrate A implementation entirely."

report.summary: Atlas plugin hook audit complete: 13 hooks wired across 8 event types (not 8 hooks as expected; count includes all command invocations). All 9 scripts verified present. Dispatch tripwire and completion gate both fail-open by design. Prompt optimizer augments prompt via additionalContext, trigger-gated by default, fails open. Dispatch monitoring uses hardcoded "Agent" and "Task" matchers. No structural wiring defects detected.

PostToolUse: 2 (format_after_edit.py, dispatch_tripwire.py). Stop: 3 (completion_gate.py, ingest_session.py, nudge.py). SubagentStop: 2 (ingest_session.py, nudge.py). SessionEnd: 1 (ingest_session.py). PreCompact: 1 (ingest_session.py). Total: 13 command hooks across 8 event types. Standing-consent orchestration contract properly isolated to atlas-engine per criterion (e). Reference loading follows trigger model per criterion (c). Discovery delegation strong across all skills. Criteria (b), (d), (f), (g) show no violations.

refuted.claim: references-resolution report: 81 dangling internal file references; the references/ directory exists but is completely empty; all 75+ references to references/*.md are broken (10 SKILL.md HIGH, 18 command MEDIUM).
refuted.reason: Checked the wrong directory. The empty dir it inspected is the vestigial top-level plugins/atlas/references/. Actual reference files live in skill-relative subdirs: `ls skills/atlas-engine/references/` returns all 19 files it flagged missing (operating-contract, workflow-template, capability-catalog, docs-ssot, multi-stage-planning, capability-routing, subagent-kit, scaffolding, hooks-automation, verification-and-grounding, session-lifecycle, memory-access, prompt-optimization, ux-test-swarm, execution-testing, lsp-and-symbols, claude-code-tuning, codeql, pytest-coverage), and skills/atlas-expedition/references/ has the other 6 plus roles/ (all 6 role files). Skills cite these relative to their own SKILL.md; the claim assumed top-level resolution.

refuted.claim: references-resolution report: references/operating-contract.md is missing but referenced by 14 command files at line 9.
refuted.reason: The report truncated the citation. Actual atlas-component.md:9 reads `cat "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/atlas-engine/references/operating-contract.md"` and line 12 gives the same skill-relative fallback path. That file exists (3456 bytes). The fully-qualified CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT path resolves; there is no dangling command reference.

refuted.claim: references-resolution report: 6 references/roles/*.md files missing; directory references/roles/ does not exist.
refuted.reason: Directory exists at skills/atlas-expedition/references/roles/ with all six files present (browser-persona.md, calc-verifier.md, cartographer.md, fuzz-boundary.md, scripted-persona.md, synthesis-reporter.md). atlas-expedition cites them relative to its own skill dir.

refuted.claim: routing-spine report: agent api-usage-map is completely orphaned - no command references, no skill references, not mentioned anywhere; candidate for removal.
refuted.reason: Not zero references. grep finds it in README.md:56 (agent roster) and output-styles/atlas-orchestrator.md:35 (dispatchable agent roster). It is unrouted from commands, but 'completely orphaned / no references anywhere' is false.

refuted.claim: inventory report (HIGH): atlas-grafana.md command divergence is an actionable defect - present in installed copy, absent from repo.
refuted.reason: The divergence is an intentional, committed removal, not a defect. `git log --oneline -- commands/atlas-grafana.md` shows commit bbd6adb 'feat(atlas): remove outdated Grafana command documentation'. The repo is the intended state; the installed cache (lastUpdated 2026-07-06) is merely stale and self-heals on reinstall. Nothing to fix in the repo.

refuted.claim: inventory report: plugin.json lacks mcpServers/bundle configuration - potential gap.
refuted.reason: By design, not a gap. The connectors report and plugin.json's own description confirm atlas ships zero vendor MCP connectors; the ten .mcpb bundles live in the four owning domain plugins. Absence of an mcpServers block in atlas is the intended posture.

refuted.claim: ux-duplication report (HIGH): atlas-expedition references missing harness infrastructure (docs/claude_testers/harness/, run_all.sh, datagen.py) and this blocks Substrate A entirely.
refuted.reason: These are runtime-generated artifacts, not shipped plugin files. atlas-expedition/SKILL.md:23 states writes land under docs/claude_testers/harness/ (a per-run output dir), and personas.md describes datagen.py as generated into RUN_DIR at execution time. Their absence from the repo is expected. Whether the generation logic is complete is a separate [unverified] question, but 'missing shipped infrastructure blocks the skill' is not supported.

stages.title: Reference-integrity cross-check (prove zero dangling references/*.md)
stages.artifact: A resolved-vs-dangling reference report covering every references/*.md citation in skills/ and commands/, resolved skill-relative (or via CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT for commands).
stages.check: Script iterates each citation, resolves against its containing skill dir or CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/skills path, asserts the target exists; exits nonzero on any miss. Expected: 0 missing. CONCURRENT (depends_on empty). This converts the refuted 81-dangling claim into a proven gate.

stages.title: Hook-load cross-check (eight hooks load, fail-open)
stages.artifact: Confirmation list: 8 hook scripts in hooks/ (session_boot, prompt_optimizer, bash_advisor, format_after_edit, dispatch_tripwire, completion_gate, ingest_session, nudge), each compiling and wired in hooks.json, plus atlas_doctor.py wired as a script-hook.
stages.check: json.load(hooks.json) parses; py_compile each of the 8 hooks/ scripts succeeds; grep confirms try/except fail-open in dispatch_tripwire.py + completion_gate.py + prompt_optimizer.py. Expected: 8 present, all compile. CONCURRENT.

stages.title: Connector-inert cross-check (ten connectors inert, atlas ships zero)
stages.artifact: Inventory confirming 10 vendor .mcpb across it-operations/security-compliance/microsoft-365/hr-payroll, atlas has no .mcp.json/userConfig, and no hardcoded secrets in vendor .mcp.json.
stages.check: find yields 10 .mcpb; grep atlas for userConfig/.mcp.json = 0; grep vendor .mcp.json for literal CLIENT_ID/SECRET/API_KEY values = 0 (only ${user_config.*}). CONCURRENT.

stages.check: for f in agents/*.md; grep -qi 'Report back' -> records 4 MISSING of 18. This is the reproducible RED state for the three-tier loop. CONCURRENT.

stages.check: Re-run Stage 4 check -> 0 MISSING across all 18 agents; git diff touches only the 4 files. Behind user-approval gate. Loop-back: if Stage 6 rejects a schema, re-run Stage 4 then redo this stage.

stages.check: grep the Step 2 table -> 3 new hits, one per skill; git diff shows only additions, existing rows byte-identical. Behind approval gate. CONCURRENT with 8,9.

stages.check: grep confirms atlas-expedition named canonical in both capability-routing.md and atlas-engine/SKILL.md; ux-test-swarm.md retains its CANONICAL HOME banner; grep commands/ for ux-cartographer|ux-persona|ux-fuzzer|ux-accuracy-oracle|ux-reporter = 0. Approval gate. Deletion of the 5 agents is a separate gated removal candidate, not this stage.

stages.artifact: Recorded decision on canonical upstream plus the matching edit to atlas_doctor.py expected URL and/or .kimi-plugin/marketplace.json so both agree.
stages.check: python3 scripts/atlas_doctor.py -> 0 PROBLEM(S) (marketplace-source and clone-remote both PASS). Approval gate AND blocked until the open question on canonical upstream is answered.

stages.title: [GATED] Reconcile uncommitted marketplace.json + README.md with CHANGELOG

stages.check: git diff --stat clean after commit; CHANGELOG names the change; atlas_doctor still reports 0 problems. Approval gate. Loop-back: if Stage 10 changes URL strategy, re-run this stage's diff.

stages.check: Directory exists with each named artifact; CHANGELOG references the audit dir; every cross-check artifact (stages 1,2,3,4,6) present and non-empty. Final DoD gate.

removal_candidates.evidence: `ls references/` returns empty (0 files) in both repo and installed copy; every actual references/*.md citation resolves skill-relative (skills/<name>/references/) or via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/atlas-engine/references/. No citation resolves to this top-level dir.
removal_candidates.blast_radius: None - nothing routes to it; deleting it changes no reference and is the source of the refuted 81-dangling false alarm.

removal_candidates.item: The five ux-* agent specs (agents/ux-cartographer.md, ux-persona.md, ux-fuzzer.md, ux-accuracy-oracle.md, ux-reporter.md)
removal_candidates.evidence: ux-test-swarm.md:3-7 CANONICAL HOME banner points all UX runs to atlas-expedition; capability-routing.md:23 routes UX solely to atlas-expedition; no command dispatches them; atlas-expedition/references/roles/ already provides the six role prompts that supersede them.
removal_candidates.blast_radius: Medium - referenced in atlas-engine/SKILL.md:150, the atlas-orchestrator output-style roster, and ux-test-swarm.md; removal requires striking those in-place references first (Stage 8 marks them reference-only as the non-destructive alternative).

removal_candidates.evidence: Unrouted from every command; only two references, both roster mentions (README.md:56, output-styles/atlas-orchestrator.md:35). NOT completely orphaned (refutes routing-spine).
removal_candidates.blast_radius: Low-medium - removal needs edits to the README and output-style rosters; but it is a DB-audit companion agent with latent value, so keep-and-fix (add Report-back schema in Stage 5) is likely preferable to deletion.

removal_candidates.evidence: Inventory report lists them as repo-only build/tool caches diverging from installed state.
removal_candidates.blast_radius: None (regenerable caches) - prefer gitignoring them (Stage 9) over deletion so they stop being tracked without disrupting local tooling.

plan.loop_demo_candidate: Add 'Report back' structured-return schemas to the four nonconformant agent specs (api-usage-map.md, schema-inventory.md, naming-glossary-audit.md, rls-privilege-audit.md). Best three-tier demo because it is fully self-contained (no upstream decision, unlike the marketplace-URL fix), bounded to four small files, and backed by a crisp automatable binary check: `for f in agents/*.md; do grep -qi 'Report back' \"$f\"; done` reproduces a clean RED state (4 of 18 MISSING) now, the fix drives it to 0 MISSING, and an independent-context verifier re-runs the same grep plus spot-reads the added named fields - red reproduction, fix, and verification each in a separate context (Stages 4 -> 5 -> 6). It also directly satisfies mission goal 3 (every dispatch names an exact return schema).

gaps.gap: Unresolved dangling agent references in a goal-6 surface. capability-routing.md rows 18-19 dispatch `codebase-explorer`, `Explore`, and `Plan` (confirmed: 2 grep hits), none of which exist in agents/ (18 files, none match). The routing-spine Tier 1 report raised this exact finding and NO stage addresses it. Stage 1 is the only reference-integrity gate and its scope is explicitly 'every references/*.md citation in skills/ and commands/' - it does not resolve agent-name references in the routing table, nor references in agents/, hooks.json, or plugin.json, all of which mission goal 6 names as surfaces that must be dangling-free. DoD requires 'all dangling refs resolved or struck'; this class is never checked.
gaps.fix: Add a stage (or widen Stage 1) that resolves agent-name references in capability-routing.md against agents/ plus the global/external agent registry, and either confirms Explore/Plan/codebase-explorer resolve to real global agents or annotates them as external. Extend the gate to cover agents/, hooks.json, and plugin.json references so goal 6's full surface is proven, not just references/*.md.

gaps.gap: Mission goal 5 (reduced hallucinations) has no verification stage and coverage is demonstrably partial. Briefs must permit 'I don't know', require citations, and mark [unverified]. Grep shows only 9 of 18 agent specs contain any such language; 9 lack it entirely (completeness-critic, db-prober, explorer, naming-glossary-audit, rls-privilege-audit, schema-inventory, ui-runtime-tester, verifier, and one more). The fable5-scan reported violations only in criterion (a) and never flagged goal 5, so either goal 5 is uncovered entirely or the scan's silent 'clean' on that criterion was itself wrong. No stage enforces or even inventories this.
gaps.fix: Add a conformance stage that greps all 18 agent specs (and skill dispatch briefs) for the three hallucination controls, records the RED set of nonconformant specs, and - behind the removal/edit approval gate - adds the missing 'permit I don't know / cite sources / mark [unverified]' language. List any residue [unverified] in the Stage 12 rollup.

gaps.gap: Mission goal 4 (four-part briefs) rests on an unverified Tier 1 claim with no resolving stage. The fable5-scan explicitly marked criterion (f) UNVERIFIED ('does not explicitly state prior-context in the prompt template - unclear if implicit context suffices'). The four-part language exists only in the template files (multi-stage-planning.md, subagent-kit.md), not confirmed across the actual dispatch examples in the skills. No stage (4-6 only exercise criterion a) verifies four-part brief shape end to end.
gaps.fix: Add a check that every dispatch example in the orchestrating skills contains all four brief parts (task, product, where-to-save, prior-context), or explicitly list the shortfall as [unverified] residue in Stage 12.

gaps.gap: Stage 2's check cannot fail on its load-bearing claim. Goal 2 requires hooks 'gate on the orchestration marker and fail open'. Stage 2 verifies only py_compile (syntactic validity) plus a grep for try/except tokens in dispatch_tripwire/completion_gate/prompt_optimizer. A grep for 'try/except' passes whenever the tokens appear anywhere, even if the except re-raises or sits outside the exit path, and py_compile proves nothing about runtime behavior. Neither confirms (a) the gate actually keys on the is_orchestrating marker before blocking, nor (b) that the failure path actually exits 0.
gaps.fix: Strengthen Stage 2 to execute each hook with a synthetic payload that forces an internal exception and assert exit code 0 (real fail-open), and assert completion_gate/dispatch_tripwire do NOT block when the orchestration marker is absent and DO when present - a check that can actually go red.

gaps.gap: Goal 2 'standing-consent contract owned solely by atlas-engine' has no verification stage. It is asserted only by a single-context fable5-scan grep. (Spot-check found the language in atlas-engine/SKILL.md and multi-stage-planning.md, both atlas-engine-owned, so it currently holds - but nothing in the stage map gates it, so a future edit could reintroduce duplication undetected.)
gaps.fix: Add a grep assertion (co-locate with Stage 8's ownership check) that standing-consent language appears only under skills/atlas-engine/, exiting nonzero on any hit elsewhere.

gaps.gap: Removal gating produces no recorded STOP/approval or explicit-deferral artifact for the ranked candidates. DoD: 'the orchestrator STOPS for user approval before any delete/move/rename.' Removal candidate rank 1 (empty top-level references/ dir - the source of the refuted 81-dangling false alarm) and rank 3 (api-usage-map) are never deleted by any stage, which is correct, but no stage records the keep/defer decision. The empty references/ dir persists in the tree with nothing striking it or documenting why it stays.
gaps.fix: Make the STOP-for-approval an explicit stage that presents the ranked removal table and records the user's per-candidate decision (delete/defer/keep) into the Stage 12 decision records, including an explicit disposition for the vestigial references/ dir.

gaps.gap: The canonical UX owner's runtime generation logic is never verified. Stage 8 names atlas-expedition sole UX owner, and the ux-duplication refutation conceded 'whether the generation logic is complete is a separate [unverified] question' for run_all.sh/datagen.py (generated into RUN_DIR). No stage confirms atlas-expedition can actually produce that harness, so the sole surviving UX substrate could be non-functional at execution time.
gaps.fix: Either add a stage that traces the generation logic in atlas-expedition/personas.md and SKILL.md to confirm it emits run_all.sh/datagen.py, or list harness-generation completeness as explicit [unverified] residue in Stage 12.
